summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the workflow import URL endpoint can be used to make the n8n server fetch content from an internal-only HTTP service and return it through the API response, demonstrating server-side request forgery.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the n8n server to fetch content from the internal metadata server at http://127.0.0.1:9999 and return it through the API response body, demonstrating that the server will make arbitrary outbound requests to internal addresses on behalf of an authenticated user.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started n8n, created an owner account, logged in, and then called GET /rest/workflows/from-url with an internal-only URL. The n8n server fetched the response from the internal server at 127.0.0.1:9999 and returned its content through the API, confirming the SSRF.
verification_method: The verify script starts a mock internal HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:9999 that returns a JSON response containing the proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_7f3a9b2c embedded in a valid n8n workflow node. The script then calls the from-url endpoint with http://127.0.0.1:9999/metadata as the URL parameter. If the proof string appears in the API response body, the SSRF is confirmed. This check distinguishes real server-side request forgery from a generic error, a cached response, or a client-side request.
evidence_produced_by_run: The API response body contains the proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_7f3a9b2c embedded in the workflow node data, confirming the server fetched content from the internal-only service.
The internal server log shows an incoming GET request from the n8n server process, proving the request originated server-side.
The endpoint returned HTTP 200 with the internal server's full response data, not a connection error, validation failure, or redirect.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
